_notes: BLOG CATEGORY LIBRARY — derived from services.json (the city pages' services list).
THE RULE: services.json LEADS, this file FOLLOWS. When a service is added,
renamed, or removed in services.json, update THIS file to match — never the
other way around. The generator enforces this: every service slug in
services.json must appear in exactly one category's 'services' list below,
or `python3 _generator/generate.py` prints a loud WARNING naming the
uncovered slug. Stale slugs (listed here but gone from services.json) are
warned about too. So a mismatch can never slip through silently.
Grouping style (owner's direction, July 2026): categories are GENERAL —
several related services share one category (e.g. Faucet Installation,
Faucet Repair, and Faucet Replacement are all just 'Faucets'), never 1:1
service:category. A new service usually joins an existing category; only
genuinely new territory earns a new one.
Each blog post in blog-posts.json carries a 'category' field matching a
'name' below. Posts with a missing or unknown category build as 'General'
(with a warning) rather than breaking. Categories appear on the archive
page (docs/blog/all-posts.html) as section headings, in the order below —
only categories that actually have posts are shown.
